fault_hypothesis: Lines 72-73 incorrectly remove the last element when both streams share the same trailing value, which is not a valid deduplication step for a merge operation. This causes duplicate tail values to be wrongly popped from the output.

constraints.intent: After the pop, the output length must still equal the sum of both input lengths; popping here violates this invariant.
After executing the pop branch, output count must equal total input count.

spec.expr: not (out__1 and left and right and out__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1]) or len(out__1) > len(left) + len(right) - 1

constraints.intent: Before the conditional pop, the shared-tail condition should not trigger a removal when both inputs legitimately end with the same value.
The returned merged list must contain exactly as many elements as both inputs combined.
